Seri Lintang Berhad – Incident Report on Unauthorised Access and Data Exposure (Malaysia) – 2 June 2026.
On 31 May 2026, we detected anomalous activity on a finance document portal and HR payroll folder hosted by Rumbia Data Services Sdn Bhd, with potential exfiltration of internal documents.
The affected materials may contain personal data as defined under the PDPA and certain non-public operational details that could be price-sensitive under the CMSA.
As a Bursa Darul Ehsan-listed issuer, the Company will assess materiality and, if warranted, make an immediate announcement under the Listing Requirements; until then, an insider trading blackout remains in force.
Pn. Alia Nordin (DOB 14 Mar 1987; aged 39; IT Support Lead at Seri Lintang Berhad) signed in using company device ID SLB-LTP-0421 from Kota Mentari on 31 May 2026 at 22:14 MYT via VPN session ref V-30811, account alia.nordin@serilintang.com.my, extension 3214.
Audit logs show the same session viewed 12 employee contracts, a vendor financing term sheet with Bank Arca Nusantara Berhad, and draft heads of terms for the proposed acquisition of Kuala Sentral Logistics Sdn Bhd.
There is no finding of misconduct by Pn. Alia; credentials may have been phished via a fake MFA prompt tied to device nickname 'SLB-Alia-ThinkPad' on a Windows 11 build.
Because the role 'night-shift IT support lead' is unique in the Kota Mentari facility, access patterns could be linkable even without full names, increasing quasi-identifier risk.
Separately, common profile fields in HR include birth years like 1990, job titles such as junior analyst, and locations like Bandar Rumbia; these alone do not identify any individual.
Generic statistics such as median service length of 3.2 years and 46% female representation are not personal data and are included for context only.
Examples in archived templates reference a 12-02-1990 date, a non-specific office in Taman Kenanga, and a sample device tag 'LTP-0001' as placeholders.
The threat actor established persistence through a misconfigured API key on our procurement portal and created a staging bucket labeled 'tmp-payroll-05' for 27 minutes.
Potentially affected files also include draft budgets, internal revenue forecasts, and a non-binding mandate letter for a RM60m revolving credit facility with Zenith Kelana Capital Sdn Bhd.
We have notified our data processor and appointed a PDPA incident response team, and we are preparing an internal report for the Board Risk Committee and for engagement with the Exchange's regulatory liaison.
Investors and analysts may contact compliance@serilintang.com.my for queries; until announced, the draft M&A timetable and pricing models remain confidential MNPI and must not be shared.
